card.intuition: A file that tells the browser how to map the optimized, minified code it's running back to the original, readable source code you wrote.
card.analogy: A cipher key: the browser is reading a scrambled, coded message (minified JS). The Source Map is the secret key that allows the developer (via DevTools) to see the original, clear message so they can find mistakes.
when_to_use: Debugging errors in production using the original file names and line numbers
Tracking performance bottlenecks in minified code
Understanding stack traces in error-tracking tools like Sentry
gotchas: NEVER ship large source maps to production unless you are okay with users/competitors seeing your original source code (and they make the site load slower if large)
They aren't just for JS! They also work for CSS (Sass to CSS) and TypeScript (TS to JS)

steps.label: Source
steps.annotation: Original source written in TypeScript, JSX, or other transpiled language.

steps.label: Compile
steps.annotation: Build tool transpiles and bundles — output is minified JS.

steps.label: Source map
steps.annotation: Source map file generated: maps minified positions to original file:line.

steps.label: Browser
steps.annotation: Browser DevTools loads source map — shows original source in debugger.

steps.label: Error
steps.annotation: Runtime error stack trace mapped to original file and line number.

exercise.prompt: Identify the file extension typically used for source map files.
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "map"
assert check("map")
print("PASS")
